# loguru-themes

> Curated color themes (Dracula, Nord, Catppuccin, Monokai, plus neutral dark/light)
> and minimalist Unicode level icons for the `loguru` logging library, applied to any
> logger in a single call: `apply_theme(logger, "dracula")`. A theme also acts as a
> full terminal color scheme — loguru's native color tags (`<red>`, `<blue>`, …)
> follow the theme's palette.

Key facts for using the library:

- Primary entry point: `apply_theme(logger, theme, *, icons=True, sink=None, level="TRACE", colorize=None, replace=True, remap_colors=True)`.
- Built-in theme names: `dracula`, `nord`, `catppuccin`, `monokai`, `dark`, `light` (case-insensitive; `list_themes()` lists them; `ThemeName` is a `Literal` for autocomplete).
- Themes are immutable and customizable via chainable `with_*` helpers (`with_color`, `with_icon`, `with_icons`, `with_fg`, `with_uniform_message`, `with_level`, `with_palette`, …).
- Per-level icons are plain Unicode; override with `icons=False`, a full `IconSet`, or a mapping like `icons={"error": "!!"}`.
- Built-in highlights: ERROR message text is red; CRITICAL is bold on a red background.
- Lower-level building blocks: `configure_levels(logger, theme)` (colors + icons, no sink) and `build_format(theme)` (the loguru `format` value) let you keep your own sinks/format.
- Color-scheme remapping is process-global; opt out with `remap_colors=False`, undo with `restore_palette()`, or scope with `using_palette(palette)`.
- Requires Python 3.9+ and `loguru>=0.7`. Install: `pip install loguru-themes`.

## Documentation

- [Getting started](/getting-started/): install and apply your first theme
- [Themes](/themes/): the built-in themes, listing, and looking them up
- [Icons](/icons/): per-level icons — disable, replace, or override inline
- [Customizing](/customizing/): tweak a theme with `with_*` and build custom themes
- [Color scheme](/color-scheme/): native loguru color tags follow the theme palette
- [Use your own logger](/own-logger/): `configure_levels` + `build_format` with your own sinks
- [API reference](/api-reference/): public functions and types

Russian translations are available under the same paths prefixed with `/ru/`
(e.g. [/ru/getting-started/](/ru/getting-started/)).

## Examples

- `examples/01_quickstart.py`: apply a theme in one line; all levels
- `examples/02_customize.py`: tweak a theme with the `with_*` API
- `examples/03_color_scheme.py`: native tags follow the palette; opt-out and scoped
